explanation: The case satisfies all Section 2 criteria. Diagnosis (2.1): cervical radiculopathy at C5-C6 with nerve root compression and dermatomal radicular symptoms is confirmed. Imaging (2.3): MRI within 6 months interpreted by a radiologist shows pathology at C5-C6 consistent with symptoms and surgical level. Conservative treatment (2.2): PT was 7 weeks (>6 weeks) directed at the cervical condition with functional outcomes; pharmacotherapy includes naproxen (NSAID) for 6 weeks and gabapentin (neuropathic agent) for 5 weeks, satisfying the requirement of at least two classes for ≥4 weeks each; one cervical ESI satisfies the interventional requirement. Physician documentation (2.4): all four required elements are provided. All criteria met, so authorization is approved.
